# google_migration_center_report

Report represents an analytical assessment report summarizing infrastructure size, costs, and target suggestions.



<div class = "oics-button" style="float: right; margin: 0 0 -15px">
  <a href="https://console.cloud.google.com/cloudshell/open?cloudshell_git_repo=https%3A%2F%2Fgithub.com%2Fterraform-google-modules%2Fdocs-examples.git&cloudshell_image=gcr.io%2Fcloudshell-images%2Fcloudshell%3Alatest&cloudshell_print=.%2Fmotd&cloudshell_tutorial=.%2Ftutorial.md&cloudshell_working_dir=migration_center_report_basic&open_in_editor=main.tf" target="_blank">
    <img alt="Open in Cloud Shell" src="//gstatic.com/cloudssh/images/open-btn.svg" style="max-height: 44px; margin: 32px auto; max-width: 100%;">
  </a>
</div>
## Example Usage - Migration Center Report Basic


```hcl
resource "google_migration_center_group" "default" {
  location = "us-central1"
  group_id = "group-test"
}

resource "google_migration_center_preference_set" "default" {
  location          = "us-central1"
  preference_set_id = "pref-set-test"
}

resource "google_migration_center_report_config" "default" {
  location         = "us-central1"
  report_config_id = "report-config-test"
  group_preferenceset_assignments {
    group          = google_migration_center_group.default.id
    preference_set = google_migration_center_preference_set.default.id
  }
}

resource "google_migration_center_report" "default" {
  location      = "us-central1"
  report_id     = "report-test"
  report_config = google_migration_center_report_config.default.report_config_id
  type          = "TOTAL_COST_OF_OWNERSHIP"
  description   = "Terraform integration test description"
  display_name  = "Terraform integration test display"
}
```

## Argument Reference

The following arguments are supported:


* `location` -
  (Required)
  Resource ID segment making up resource `name`. It identifies the resource within its parent collection as described in https://google.aip.dev/122.

* `report_config` -
  (Required)
  Resource ID segment making up resource `name`. It identifies the resource within its parent collection as described in https://google.aip.dev/122.

* `report_id` -
  (Required)
  User specified id for the report. It will become the last component
  of the report name. The id must be unique within the project, must
  conform with RFC-1034, is restricted to lower-cased letters, and has a
  maximum length of 63 characters. The id must match the regular expression:
  [a-z]([a-z0-9-]{0,61}[a-z0-9])?.


* `description` -
  (Optional)
  Free-text description.

* `display_name` -
  (Optional)
  User-friendly display name. Maximum length is 63 characters.

* `type` -
  (Optional)
  Report type.
  Possible values:
  TOTAL_COST_OF_OWNERSHIP

* `project` - (Optional) The ID of the project in which the resource belongs.
    If it is not provided, the provider project is used.

* `deletion_policy` - (Optional) Whether Terraform will be prevented from destroying the resource. Defaults to DELETE.
	When a 'terraform destroy' or 'terraform apply' would delete the resource,
	the command will fail if this field is set to "PREVENT" in Terraform state.
	When set to "ABANDON", the command will remove the resource from Terraform
	management without updating or deleting the resource in the API.
	When set to "DELETE", deleting the resource is allowed.


## Attributes Reference

In addition to the arguments listed above, the following computed attributes are exported:

* `id` - an identifier for the resource with format `projects/{{project}}/locations/{{location}}/reportConfigs/{{report_config}}/reports/{{report_id}}`

* `create_time` -
  Creation timestamp.

* `name` -
  Name of resource.

* `state` -
  Report creation state.
  Possible values:
  PENDING
  SUCCEEDED
  FAILED

* `summary` -
  Describes the Summary view of a Report, which contains aggregated values
  for all the groups and preference sets included in this Report.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_summary).

* `update_time` -
  Last update timestamp.


<a name="nested_summary"></a>The `summary` block contains:

* `all_assets_stats` -
  (Output)
  Aggregate statistics for a collection of assets.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_summary_all_assets_stats).

* `group_findings` -
  (Output)
  Findings for each Group included in this report.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_summary_group_findings).


<a name="nested_summary_all_assets_stats"></a>The `all_assets_stats` block contains:

* `core_count_histogram` -
  (Output)
  A Histogram Chart shows a distribution of values into buckets, showing
  a count of values which fall into a bucket.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_summary_all_assets_stats_core_count_histogram).

* `memory_bytes_histogram` -
  (Output)
  A Histogram Chart shows a distribution of values into buckets, showing
  a count of values which fall into a bucket.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_summary_all_assets_stats_memory_bytes_histogram).

* `memory_utilization_chart` -
  (Output)
  Utilization Chart is a specific type of visualization which displays
  a metric classified into "Used" and "Free" buckets.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_summary_all_assets_stats_memory_utilization_chart).

* `operating_system` -
  (Output)
  Describes a collection of data points rendered as a Chart.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_summary_all_assets_stats_operating_system).

* `software_instances` -
  (Output)
  Describes a collection of data points rendered as a Chart.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_summary_all_assets_stats_software_instances).

* `storage_bytes_histogram` -
  (Output)
  A Histogram Chart shows a distribution of values into buckets, showing
  a count of values which fall into a bucket.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_summary_all_assets_stats_storage_bytes_histogram).

* `storage_utilization_chart` -
  (Output)
  Utilization Chart is a specific type of visualization which displays
  a metric classified into "Used" and "Free" buckets.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_summary_all_assets_stats_storage_utilization_chart).

* `total_assets` -
  (Output)
  Count of the number of unique assets in this collection.

* `total_cores` -
  (Output)
  Sum of the CPU core count of all the assets in this collection.

* `total_memory_bytes` -
  (Output)
  Sum of the memory in bytes of all the assets in this collection.

* `total_storage_bytes` -
  (Output)
  Sum of persistent storage in bytes of all the assets in this collection.


<a name="nested_summary_all_assets_stats_core_count_histogram"></a>The `core_count_histogram` block contains:

* `buckets` -
  (Output)
  Buckets in the histogram.
  There will be `n+1` buckets matching `n` lower bounds in the request.
  The first bucket will be from -infinity to the first bound.
  Subsequent buckets will be between one bound and the next.
  The final bucket will be from the final bound to infinity.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_summary_all_assets_stats_core_count_histogram_buckets).


<a name="nested_summary_all_assets_stats_core_count_histogram_buckets"></a>The `buckets` block contains:

* `count` -
  (Output)
  Count of items in the bucket.

* `lower_bound` -
  (Output)
  Lower bound - inclusive.

* `upper_bound` -
  (Output)
  Upper bound - exclusive.

<a name="nested_summary_all_assets_stats_memory_bytes_histogram"></a>The `memory_bytes_histogram` block contains:

* `buckets` -
  (Output)
  Buckets in the histogram.
  There will be `n+1` buckets matching `n` lower bounds in the request.
  The first bucket will be from -infinity to the first bound.
  Subsequent buckets will be between one bound and the next.
  The final bucket will be from the final bound to infinity.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_summary_all_assets_stats_memory_bytes_histogram_buckets).


<a name="nested_summary_all_assets_stats_memory_bytes_histogram_buckets"></a>The `buckets` block contains:

* `count` -
  (Output)
  Count of items in the bucket.

* `lower_bound` -
  (Output)
  Lower bound - inclusive.

* `upper_bound` -
  (Output)
  Upper bound - exclusive.

<a name="nested_summary_all_assets_stats_memory_utilization_chart"></a>The `memory_utilization_chart` block contains:

* `free` -
  (Output)
  Aggregate value which falls into the "Free" bucket.

* `used` -
  (Output)
  Aggregate value which falls into the "Used" bucket.

<a name="nested_summary_all_assets_stats_operating_system"></a>The `operating_system` block contains:

* `data_points` -
  (Output)
  Each data point in the chart is represented as a name-value pair
  with the name being the x-axis label, and the value being the y-axis
  value.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_summary_all_assets_stats_operating_system_data_points).


<a name="nested_summary_all_assets_stats_operating_system_data_points"></a>The `data_points` block contains:

* `label` -
  (Output)
  The X-axis label for this data point.

* `value` -
  (Output)
  The Y-axis value for this data point.

<a name="nested_summary_group_findings_preference_set_findings"></a>The `preference_set_findings` block contains:

* `compute_engine_finding` -
  (Output)
  A set of findings that applies to assets destined for Compute Engine.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_summary_group_findings_preference_set_findings_compute_engine_finding).

* `description` -
  (Output)
  Description for the Preference Set.

* `display_name` -
  (Output)
  Display Name of the Preference Set

* `machine_preferences` -
  (Output)
  VirtualMachinePreferences enables you to create sets of assumptions, for
  example, a geographical location and pricing track, for your migrated virtual
  machines. The set of preferences influence recommendations for migrating
  virtual machine assets.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_summary_group_findings_preference_set_findings_machine_preferences).

* `monthly_cost_compute` -
  (Output)
  Represents an amount of money with its currency type.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_summary_group_findings_preference_set_findings_monthly_cost_compute).

* `monthly_cost_network_egress` -
  (Output)
  Represents an amount of money with its currency type.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_summary_group_findings_preference_set_findings_monthly_cost_network_egress).

* `monthly_cost_os_license` -
  (Output)
  Represents an amount of money with its currency type.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_summary_group_findings_preference_set_findings_monthly_cost_os_license).

* `monthly_cost_other` -
  (Output)
  Represents an amount of money with its currency type.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_summary_group_findings_preference_set_findings_monthly_cost_other).

* `monthly_cost_storage` -
  (Output)
  Represents an amount of money with its currency type.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_summary_group_findings_preference_set_findings_monthly_cost_storage).

* `monthly_cost_total` -
  (Output)
  Represents an amount of money with its currency type.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_summary_group_findings_preference_set_findings_monthly_cost_total).

* `sole_tenant_finding` -
  (Output)
  A set of findings that applies to assets destined for Sole-Tenant nodes.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_summary_group_findings_preference_set_findings_sole_tenant_finding).

* `vmware_engine_finding` -
  (Output)
  A set of findings that applies to assets destined for VMWare Engine.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_summary_group_findings_preference_set_findings_vmware_engine_finding).


<a name="nested_summary_group_findings_preference_set_findings_compute_engine_finding"></a>The `compute_engine_finding` block contains:

* `allocated_asset_count` -
  (Output)
  Count of assets which were allocated.

* `allocated_disk_types` -
  (Output)
  Set of disk types allocated to assets.

* `allocated_regions` -
  (Output)
  Set of regions in which the assets were allocated.

* `machine_series_allocations` -
  (Output)
  Distribution of assets based on the Machine Series.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_summary_group_findings_preference_set_findings_compute_engine_finding_machine_series_allocations).


<a name="nested_summary_group_findings_preference_set_findings_compute_engine_finding_machine_series_allocations"></a>The `machine_series_allocations` block contains:

* `allocated_asset_count` -
  (Output)
  Count of assets allocated to this machine series.

* `machine_series` -
  (Output)
  A machine series, for a target product
  (e.g. Compute Engine, Google Cloud VMware Engine).
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_summary_group_findings_preference_set_findings_compute_engine_finding_machine_series_allocations_machine_series).


<a name="nested_summary_group_findings_preference_set_findings_compute_engine_finding_machine_series_allocations_machine_series"></a>The `machine_series` block contains:

* `code` -
  (Output)
  Code to identify a machine series.
  Consult this for more details on the available series for Compute Engine:
  https://cloud.google.com/compute/docs/machine-resource#machine_type_comparison
  Consult this for more details on the available series for Google Cloud
  VMware Engine:
  https://cloud.google.com/vmware-engine/pricing

<a name="nested_summary_group_findings_preference_set_findings_machine_preferences"></a>The `machine_preferences` block contains:

* `commitment_plan` -
  (Output)
  Possible values:
  COMMITMENT_PLAN_NONE
  COMMITMENT_PLAN_ONE_YEAR
  COMMITMENT_PLAN_THREE_YEARS

* `compute_engine_preferences` -
  (Output)
  The user preferences relating to Compute Engine target platform.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_summary_group_findings_preference_set_findings_machine_preferences_compute_engine_preferences).

* `region_preferences` -
  (Output)
  The user preferences relating to target regions.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_summary_group_findings_preference_set_findings_machine_preferences_region_preferences).

* `sizing_optimization_strategy` -
  (Output)
  Possible values:
  SIZING_OPTIMIZATION_STRATEGY_SAME_AS_SOURCE
  SIZING_OPTIMIZATION_STRATEGY_MODERATE
  SIZING_OPTIMIZATION_STRATEGY_AGGRESSIVE

* `sole_tenancy_preferences` -
  (Output)
  Preferences concerning Sole Tenancy nodes and VMs.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_summary_group_findings_preference_set_findings_machine_preferences_sole_tenancy_preferences).

* `target_product` -
  (Output)
  Possible values:
  COMPUTE_MIGRATION_TARGET_PRODUCT_COMPUTE_ENGINE
  COMPUTE_MIGRATION_TARGET_PRODUCT_VMWARE_ENGINE
  COMPUTE_MIGRATION_TARGET_PRODUCT_SOLE_TENANCY

* `vmware_engine_preferences` -
  (Output)
  The user preferences relating to Google Cloud VMware Engine target platform.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_summary_group_findings_preference_set_findings_machine_preferences_vmware_engine_preferences).


<a name="nested_summary_group_findings_preference_set_findings_machine_preferences_compute_engine_preferences"></a>The `compute_engine_preferences` block contains:

* `license_type` -
  (Output)
  Possible values:
  LICENSE_TYPE_DEFAULT
  LICENSE_TYPE_BRING_YOUR_OWN_LICENSE

* `machine_preferences` -
  (Output)
  The type of machines to consider when calculating virtual machine migration
  insights and recommendations.
  Not all machine types are available in all zones and regions.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_summary_group_findings_preference_set_findings_machine_preferences_compute_engine_preferences_machine_preferences).

* `persistent_disk_type` -
  (Output)
  Possible values:
  PERSISTENT_DISK_TYPE_STANDARD
  PERSISTENT_DISK_TYPE_BALANCED
  PERSISTENT_DISK_TYPE_SSD


<a name="nested_summary_group_findings_preference_set_findings_machine_preferences_compute_engine_preferences_machine_preferences"></a>The `machine_preferences` block contains:

* `allowed_machine_series` -
  (Output)
  Compute Engine machine series to consider for insights and recommendations.
  If empty, no restriction is applied on the machine series.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_summary_group_findings_preference_set_findings_machine_preferences_compute_engine_preferences_machine_preferences_allowed_machine_series).


<a name="nested_summary_group_findings_preference_set_findings_machine_preferences_compute_engine_preferences_machine_preferences_allowed_machine_series"></a>The `allowed_machine_series` block contains:

* `code` -
  (Output)
  Code to identify a machine series.
  Consult this for more details on the available series for Compute Engine:
  https://cloud.google.com/compute/docs/machine-resource#machine_type_comparison
  Consult this for more details on the available series for Google Cloud
  VMware Engine:
  https://cloud.google.com/vmware-engine/pricing

<a name="nested_summary_group_findings_preference_set_findings_machine_preferences_region_preferences"></a>The `region_preferences` block contains:

* `preferred_regions` -
  (Output)
  A list of preferred regions,
  ordered by the most preferred region first.
  Set only valid Google Cloud region names.
  See https://cloud.google.com/compute/docs/regions-zones
  for available regions.

<a name="nested_summary_group_findings_preference_set_findings_machine_preferences_sole_tenancy_preferences"></a>The `sole_tenancy_preferences` block contains:

* `commitment_plan` -
  (Output)
  Commitment plan to consider when calculating costs for virtual machine
  insights and recommendations.
  If you are unsure which value to set, a 3 year commitment plan is often a
  good value to start with.
  Possible values:
  ON_DEMAND
  COMMITMENT_1_YEAR
  COMMITMENT_3_YEAR

* `cpu_overcommit_ratio` -
  (Output)
  CPU overcommit ratio.
  Acceptable values are between 1.0 and 2.0 inclusive.

* `host_maintenance_policy` -
  (Output)
  Sole Tenancy nodes maintenance policy.
  Possible values:
  HOST_MAINTENANCE_POLICY_DEFAULT
  HOST_MAINTENANCE_POLICY_RESTART_IN_PLACE
  HOST_MAINTENANCE_POLICY_MIGRATE_WITHIN_NODE_GROUP

* `node_types` -
  (Output)
  A list of sole tenant node types.
  An empty list means that all possible node types will be considered.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_summary_group_findings_preference_set_findings_machine_preferences_sole_tenancy_preferences_node_types).


<a name="nested_summary_group_findings_preference_set_findings_machine_preferences_sole_tenancy_preferences_node_types"></a>The `node_types` block contains:

* `node_name` -
  (Output)
  Name of the Sole Tenant node. Consult
  https://cloud.google.com/compute/docs/nodes/sole-tenant-nodes

<a name="nested_summary_group_findings_preference_set_findings_machine_preferences_vmware_engine_preferences"></a>The `vmware_engine_preferences` block contains:

* `commitment_plan` -
  (Output)
  Commitment plan to consider when calculating costs for virtual machine
  insights and recommendations.
  If you are unsure which value to set, a 3 year commitment plan is often a
  good value to start with.
  Possible values:
  ON_DEMAND
  COMMITMENT_1_YEAR_MONTHLY_PAYMENTS
  COMMITMENT_3_YEAR_MONTHLY_PAYMENTS
  COMMITMENT_1_YEAR_UPFRONT_PAYMENT
  COMMITMENT_3_YEAR_UPFRONT_PAYMENT

* `cpu_overcommit_ratio` -
  (Output)
  CPU overcommit ratio.
  Acceptable values are between 1.0 and 8.0, with 0.1 increment.

* `memory_overcommit_ratio` -
  (Output)
  Memory overcommit ratio.
  Acceptable values are 1.0, 1.25, 1.5, 1.75 and 2.0.

* `storage_deduplication_compression_ratio` -
  (Output)
  The Deduplication and Compression ratio is based on the logical (Used
  Before) space required to store data before applying deduplication and
  compression, in relation to the physical (Used After) space required after
  applying deduplication and compression. Specifically, the ratio is the Used
  Before space divided by the Used After space. For example, if the Used
  Before space is 3 GB, but the physical Used After space is 1 GB, the
  deduplication and compression ratio is 3x. Acceptable values are
  between 1.0 and 4.0.

<a name="nested_summary_group_findings_preference_set_findings_monthly_cost_compute"></a>The `monthly_cost_compute` block contains:

* `currency_code` -
  (Output)
  The three-letter currency code defined in ISO 4217.

* `nanos` -
  (Output)
  Number of nano (10^-9) units of the amount.
  The value must be between -999,999,999 and +999,999,999 inclusive.
  If `units` is positive, `nanos` must be positive or zero.
  If `units` is zero, `nanos` can be positive, zero, or negative.
  If `units` is negative, `nanos` must be negative or zero.
  For example $-1.75 is represented as `units`=-1 and `nanos`=-750,000,000.

* `units` -
  (Output)
  The whole units of the amount.
  For example if `currencyCode` is `"USD"`, then 1 unit is one US dollar.

<a name="nested_summary_group_findings_preference_set_findings_monthly_cost_network_egress"></a>The `monthly_cost_network_egress` block contains:

* `currency_code` -
  (Output)
  The three-letter currency code defined in ISO 4217.

* `nanos` -
  (Output)
  Number of nano (10^-9) units of the amount.
  The value must be between -999,999,999 and +999,999,999 inclusive.
  If `units` is positive, `nanos` must be positive or zero.
  If `units` is zero, `nanos` can be positive, zero, or negative.
  If `units` is negative, `nanos` must be negative or zero.
  For example $-1.75 is represented as `units`=-1 and `nanos`=-750,000,000.

* `units` -
  (Output)
  The whole units of the amount.
  For example if `currencyCode` is `"USD"`, then 1 unit is one US dollar.

<a name="nested_summary_group_findings_preference_set_findings_monthly_cost_os_license"></a>The `monthly_cost_os_license` block contains:

  (Output)
  The three-letter currency code defined in ISO 4217.

* `nanos` -

  Number of nano (10^-9) units of the amount.
  The value must be between -999,999,999 and +999,999,999 inclusive.
  If `units` is positive, `nanos` must be positive or zero.
  If `units` is zero, `nanos` can be positive, zero, or negative.
  If `units` is negative, `nanos` must be negative or zero.
  For example $-1.75 is represented as `units`=-1 and `nanos`=-750,000,000.

* `units` -
  (Output)
  The whole units of the amount.
  For example if `currencyCode` is `"USD"`, then 1 unit is one US dollar.

<a name="nested_summary_group_findings_preference_set_findings_monthly_cost_other"></a>The `monthly_cost_other` block contains:

* `currency_code` -
  (Output)
  The three-letter currency code defined in ISO 4217.

* `nanos` -
  (Output)
  Number of nano (10^-9) units of the amount.
  The value must be between -999,999,999 and +999,999,999 inclusive.
  If `units` is positive, `nanos` must be positive or zero.
  If `units` is zero, `nanos` can be positive, zero, or negative.
  If `units` is negative, `nanos` must be negative or zero.
  For example $-1.75 is represented as `units`=-1 and `nanos`=-750,000,000.

* `units` -
  (Output)
  The whole units of the amount.
  For example if `currencyCode` is `"USD"`, then 1 unit is one US dollar.

<a name="nested_summary_group_findings_preference_set_findings_monthly_cost_storage"></a>The `monthly_cost_storage` block contains:

* `currency_code` -
  (Output)
  The three-letter currency code defined in ISO 4217.

* `nanos` -
  (Output)
  Number of nano (10^-9) units of the amount.
  The value must be between -999,999,999 and +999,999,999 inclusive.
  If `units` is positive, `nanos` must be positive or zero.
  If `units` is zero, `nanos` can be positive, zero, or negative.
  If `units` is negative, `nanos` must be negative or zero.
  For example $-1.75 is represented as `units`=-1 and `nanos`=-750,000,000.

* `units` -
  (Output)
  The whole units of the amount.
  For example if `currencyCode` is `"USD"`, then 1 unit is one US dollar.

<a name="nested_summary_group_findings_preference_set_findings_monthly_cost_total"></a>The `monthly_cost_total` block contains:

* `currency_code` -
  (Output)
  The three-letter currency code defined in ISO 4217.

* `nanos` -
  (Output)
  Number of nano (10^-9) units of the amount.
  The value must be between -999,999,999 and +999,999,999 inclusive.
  If `units` is positive, `nanos` must be positive or zero.
  If `units` is zero, `nanos` can be positive, zero, or negative.
  If `units` is negative, `nanos` must be negative or zero.
  For example $-1.75 is represented as `units`=-1 and `nanos`=-750,000,000.

* `units` -
  (Output)
  The whole units of the amount.
  For example if `currencyCode` is `"USD"`, then 1 unit is one US dollar.

<a name="nested_summary_group_findings_preference_set_findings_sole_tenant_finding"></a>The `sole_tenant_finding` block contains:

* `allocated_asset_count` -
  (Output)
  Count of assets which are allocated

* `allocated_regions` -
  (Output)
  Set of regions in which the assets are allocated

* `node_allocations` -
  (Output)
  Set of per-nodetype allocation records
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_summary_group_findings_preference_set_findings_sole_tenant_finding_node_allocations).


<a name="nested_summary_group_findings_preference_set_findings_sole_tenant_finding_node_allocations"></a>The `node_allocations` block contains:

* `allocated_asset_count` -
  (Output)
  Count of assets allocated to these nodes

* `node` -
  (Output)
  A Sole Tenant node type.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_summary_group_findings_preference_set_findings_sole_tenant_finding_node_allocations_node).

* `node_count` -
  (Output)
  Count of this node type to be provisioned


<a name="nested_summary_group_findings_preference_set_findings_sole_tenant_finding_node_allocations_node"></a>The `node` block contains:

* `node_name` -
  (Output)
  Name of the Sole Tenant node. Consult
  https://cloud.google.com/compute/docs/nodes/sole-tenant-nodes

<a name="nested_summary_group_findings_preference_set_findings_vmware_engine_finding"></a>The `vmware_engine_finding` block contains:

* `allocated_asset_count` -
  (Output)
  Count of assets which are allocated

* `allocated_regions` -
  (Output)
  Set of regions in which the assets were allocated

* `node_allocations` -
  (Output)
  Set of per-nodetype allocation records
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_summary_group_findings_preference_set_findings_vmware_engine_finding_node_allocations).


<a name="nested_summary_group_findings_preference_set_findings_vmware_engine_finding_node_allocations"></a>The `node_allocations` block contains:

* `allocated_asset_count` -
  (Output)
  Count of assets allocated to these nodes

* `node_count` -
  (Output)
  Count of this node type to be provisioned

* `vmware_node` -
  (Output)
  A VMWare Engine Node
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_summary_group_findings_preference_set_findings_vmware_engine_finding_node_allocations_vmware_node).


<a name="nested_summary_group_findings_preference_set_findings_vmware_engine_finding_node_allocations_vmware_node"></a>The `vmware_node` block contains:

* `code` -
  (Output)
  Code to identify VMware Engine node series, e.g. "ve1-standard-72". Based
  on the displayName of
  cloud.google.com/vmware-engine/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.nodeTypes

## Timeouts

This resource provides the following
[Timeouts](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/plugin/sdkv2/resources/retries-and-customizable-timeouts) configuration options:

- `create` - Default is 20 minutes.
- `delete` - Default is 20 minutes.

## Import


Report can be imported using any of these accepted formats:

* `projects/{{project}}/locations/{{location}}/reportConfigs/{{report_config}}/reports/{{report_id}}`
* `{{project}}/{{location}}/{{report_config}}/{{report_id}}`
* `{{location}}/{{report_config}}/{{report_id}}`

In Terraform v1.12.0 and later, use an [`identity` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/identities) to import Report using identity values. For example:

```tf
import {
  identity = {
    location = "<-required value->"
    report_config = "<-required value->"
    reportId = "<-required value->"
    project = "<-optional value->"
  }
  to = google_migration_center_report.default
}
```

In Terraform v1.5.0 and later, use an [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) to import Report using one of the formats above. For example:

```tf
import {
  id = "projects/{{project}}/locations/{{location}}/reportConfigs/{{report_config}}/reports/{{report_id}}"
  to = google_migration_center_report.default
}
```

When using the [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import), Report can be imported using one of the formats above. For example:

```
$ terraform import google_migration_center_report.default projects/{{project}}/locations/{{location}}/reportConfigs/{{report_config}}/reports/{{report_id}}
$ terraform import google_migration_center_report.default {{project}}/{{location}}/{{report_config}}/{{report_id}}
$ terraform import google_migration_center_report.default {{location}}/{{report_config}}/{{report_id}}
```

## User Project Overrides

This resource supports [User Project Overrides](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/hashicorp/google/latest/docs/guides/provider_reference#user_project_override).
